title: Priority Stack

content.question: What are your top 3 THIS month, and can you name them without looking?
sop: Write down your top 3 priorities for this month, ranked in order.
Rate the Priority 1-10 of everything currently on your task list against that ranked list (see calculation, page 6)
Rate your actual Execution 1-10 on each of the top 3 specifically (see calculation, page 6)
Cut or delegate anything ranked below your top 3 that's eating meaningful time.
Re-stack rank weekly, priorities shift and yesterday's number one may no longer be this week's.
questions: What are your top 3 THIS month, and can you name them without looking?
If you could only finish one thing this week, would it be the thing you're working on right now?
What's currently ranked above your top priority on your calendar, even though it matters less?

definitions.def: Ordering items from most to least important, forcing an explicit priority call instead of treating everything as equally urgent.

content.equation_example: Rate Priority 1-10 (how much this actually matters) and Execution 1-10 (how well you're actually doing it). Priority 9 times Execution 3 equals 27 out of 100. Higher means high-priority work is also being executed well. Lower means either you're executing well on the wrong things, or ignoring the right ones entirely.
content.closing_statement: Treating everything as equal ranks nothing as important.

caption:
Principle Nº 155: Priority Stack

Everything is not equal.

Ask yourself: What are your top 3 THIS month, and can you name them without looking?
